compiled_state.title: Fix-Bug Behavioral Drift Prevention: Scope-Drift Reviewer + Intent-Search Enhancement
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Developers using the dso plugin rely on /dso:fix-bug to correct bugs without introducing unintended behavioral side effects. Agent-authored bug fixes are changing application behavior in ways not supported by user intent. Two failure modes: (1) the fix exceeds the bug's stated scope — adding refactoring, new abstractions, or changed defaults alongside the actual fix, and (2) the bug ticket itself contains incorrect "should" assumptions that get faithfully implemented, changing behavior that was actually correct. Existing review infrastructure (Gates 2a-2d, tiered code review) doesn't catch either case — gates are mechanical checks, and code reviewers never see the originating ticket.

## Definitions
- Behavioral scope (ticket-defined): The observable outputs, return values, control flow, defaults, and error behavior that the ticket explicitly describes as needing to change. Only the ticket defines behavioral scope; investigation findings cannot expand it.
- Technical scope (investigation-defined): The files and modules the investigation identifies as needing modification. Investigation findings expand technical scope only — they authorize touching files not mentioned in the ticket, but do not authorize changing observable behavior in those files.
- Behavioral drift: Any change to executed code paths not described in the ticket. Example: adding a null guard in a helper = permitted; changing the helper's return type = drift.
- Non-behavioral drift: Changes that do not alter executed code paths — variable renames, comment edits, formatting fixes, dead-code removal.

## Scope-Drift Reviewer Input Contract
Receives: (a) original bug ticket text, (b) investigation root_cause_report, (c) full git diff, (d) investigation-identified files. Strict read order: ticket scope extraction completes before root_cause_report is processed (PARSED_SCOPE checkpoint).

## Intent-Search Enhanced Output Contract
INTENT_CONFLICT signal: (a) ticket's behavioral claim, (b) conflicting callers with paths/snippets, (c) confidence level, (d) dependency classification (behavioral_dependency vs incidental_usage). Investigation pauses; user prompted with 3 options (confirm ticket, confirm current behavior, revise ticket).

## Success Criteria
- When a bug ticket contains an incorrect behavioral assumption and callers depend on the current behavior, the enhanced intent-search emits INTENT_CONFLICT with caller evidence and dependency classification, investigation pauses, and the user is prompted to confirm or revise the ticket before investigation proceeds
- When a fix introduces behavioral changes not described in the ticket (new logic paths, changed return values, altered control flow), the scope-drift reviewer classifies them as major drift, blocks the commit, and prompts the user for explicit approval before proceeding
- When a fix touches files identified by investigation but makes no behavioral changes in those files, the scope-drift reviewer classifies as no-drift and the fix proceeds without friction
- When a fix includes non-behavioral changes adjacent to the fix (variable renames, comment edits, dead-code removal), the scope-drift reviewer classifies as minor drift, emits a warning, and the fix proceeds without blocking
- The intent-search budget cap (currently 20 tool calls) is removed; the agent uses depth-first caller traversal with a convergence protocol (checkpoint after each caller layer, halt at high confidence, cap at 3 traversal levels)
- Both enhancements integrate into the existing fix-bug workflow: existing Gate 1a/1b/2a-2d signal contracts remain unchanged; the scope-drift reviewer emits a distinct gate signal (gate_id: scope_drift) that does not alter downstream gate parsing; the gate escalation router primary-signal counting logic is extended to include the new signal
- Validated by running /dso:fix-bug against synthetic test fixtures: (a) fix changes a return value not in ticket -> major-drift block, (b) ticket claims should return empty list but 3+ callers handle None -> INTENT_CONFLICT pause, (c) fix only touches investigation-identified files with no behavioral change -> no-drift pass

## Scenario Analysis
- Large-diff truncation (high): Reviewer must detect and flag truncated diffs rather than issuing false-clear verdicts
- Investigation over-documentation (high): Only files with explicit root-cause connection are technically authorized
- CLI_user bypass (critical): CLI_user bugs skip Gate 1a; scope-drift reviewer still runs on all fixes regardless of tag
- Missing agent file (critical): fix-bug must hard-fail when scope-drift-reviewer agent definition is missing
- Intent-search schema compatibility (high): INTENT_CONFLICT is additive alongside existing signal types
- Config key (medium): scope_drift.enabled config key (default: true)
- Depth bound (high): Convergence protocol with 3-level traversal cap replaces fixed budget
- Severity threshold (medium): Classification is structural (executed code paths vs not), not threshold-based

## Prompt Alignment Findings
- Matched signal: Agent definitions
- Prior-art patterns: Scope validation against original authorized scope; trajectory comparison for drift detection
- Behavioral review (bot-psychologist):
  - Intent-search: (1) caller dependency vs compatibility conflation -> require dependency articulation, (2) unbounded traversal -> convergence protocol with checkpoints, (3) inferring fix scope from ticket -> restrict to explicit contradictions
  - Scope-drift reviewer: (1) free-text ticket scope extraction -> PARSED_SCOPE checkpoint, (2) behavioral/non-behavioral boundary -> enumerated heuristic table, (3) investigation narrative contamination -> strict read order enforcement
  - Cross-cutting: Commit baseline derivation to explicit output block before processing contaminating inputs

## Dependencies
- fix-bug/SKILL.md gate structure (Steps 7 through Gates 2a-2d) remains stable through delivery
- dso:intent-search has no other callers; budget cap removal and schema extension have no cross-caller impact
- CLI_user skip path: enhanced intent-search does not run for CLI_user bugs; scope-drift reviewer provides partial safety net (accepted limitation)

## Approach
Dedicated dso:scope-drift-reviewer agent (sonnet) added as a new step in /dso:fix-bug between Step 7 and Gates 2a-2d, plus expansion of the existing dso:intent-search agent to validate bug behavioral claims against actual callers and API contracts.
